posts.title: What Is the Registry of Magical Practices?
posts.dek: The quiet institution behind The Monstrous Beloved is less a villain than a system with very sharp teeth.
posts.description: A spoiler-light guide to the Registry of Magical Practices, the institution connecting Selena Monroe's sapphic dark romantasy series The Monstrous Beloved.
posts.excerpt: Across The Monstrous Beloved, the Registry is the institution that decides what magic may be named, which creatures may be classified, and whose danger counts as official fact.

posts.title: Bargains, Bindings, and Consent in The Binding Night
posts.dek: A failed summoning creates the first bond in The Monstrous Beloved, but the real story is what happens when a trap becomes a choice.
posts.description: A spoiler-light worldbuilding and theme note for The Binding Night by Selena Monroe, exploring bargains, magical bindings, and consent in dark romantasy.
posts.excerpt: The Binding Night begins with a summoning mistake, but its central question is not how to escape the bond. It is whether a bond can be remade into something chosen.

posts.title: Cauldmor Research Station and the Grief Beneath The Tide Keeps
posts.dek: The coast in The Tide Keeps is not a backdrop. It is an archive, a wound, and a witness.
posts.description: A spoiler-light field note on Cauldmor Research Station, selkie mythology, grief, and archive gaps in Selena Monroe's The Tide Keeps.
posts.excerpt: Cauldmor Research Station turns scientific observation into gothic pressure: what is missing from the archive matters as much as what has been recorded.

posts.title: Dream-Feeding, Creative Obsession, and Night's Own
posts.dek: Nox feeds on dreams. Vesper writes them down. The danger begins when being known starts to feel like being chosen.
posts.description: A spoiler-light creature note for Night's Own by Selena Monroe, exploring strix mythology, dreams, creative obsession, and sapphic dark romance.
posts.excerpt: Night's Own turns inspiration into something with teeth: a strix who feeds on dreams and a novelist who begins writing toward the dark.
